narrative:
The cool, wet wedge of air that has dominated our weather since Sunday is breaking down. Morning cloudiness, with perhaps a bit of leftover drizzle southern sections, will give way to afternoon brightening and a bump-up in temperatures. Wednesday and Thursday look like dry and rather hot days as we experience quite the turnaround in weather conditions. Summertime weather continues on Friday, but there will be an improving chance for afternoon showers and thunderstorms.

This week's Monday's Message features weekend rainfall, followed by a wet/cool start to the week transitioning to full-on summer weather for Wednesday through Friday.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is here and being delivered to our retail outlets. Thanks for a record-breaking pre-publication sale! You can still avoid shipping costs with our pickup events beginning Thursday, July 2, in Boone. We'll take that to Banner Elk, West Jefferson, Spruce Pine, and Black Mountain next week; click here for details. You can see all the winning photography at our Photo Contest page . Check out volume discounts on the calendar and the "Mix and Match" sale on Drinkware.

Get the free RWC Mobile App...: Apple and Android

Forecast Discussion

A stationary front to our south today will have reducing influences on our weather. Instead, a hot dome of high pressure, centered over the Upper Midwest will curl into the area over the next couple of days, bringing rather hot conditions back to the High Country and Southwest Virginia.

Sunshine dominates Wednesday and Thursday with dry weather. Isolated pop-up showers will be restricted to areas of the Southwest North Carolina mountains.

By Friday, moisture and instability levels will be on the increase, so that hit-or-miss afternoon showers and thunderstorms will be back on the weather menu.

Over the weekend, Saturday presently looks like the greatest coverage day for afternoon showers and thunderstorms, but they will be around both days.

Early next week, a cold front will be dropping southward from the Mid-Atlantic states, stalling to our south by next Tuesday. For this reason, we will maintain widely-scattered PM shower and thundershower coverage both days.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
